items.title: Africa: AU, U.S. Envoy Discuss Aussom's Future Amid Funding Uncertainty
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- The head of the African Union Support and Stabilization Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM) met the acting U.S. ambassador to Somalia in Mogadishu to discuss the mission's future as it faces growing financial uncertainty, the AU mission said.
items.titleIt: Africa: AU, U.S. Envoy Discuss Aussom's Future Amid Funding Uncertainty

items.title: Ambassador to Senegal Opens Goreé School for Sustainable Migration
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 21 LUG - Italian Ambassador to Senegal Michele Tommasi attended the opening ceremony of the Gorée School 2026, organized as part of the project “Training, Dignity, Inclusion, and Innovation in Senegal,” funded by Italian Cooperation. Organized by the Institute for the Rights of Migrant Workers, in partnership with Inca Cgil Senegal, VIS, and numerous Italian and Senegalese institutional and social actors, the Gorée School serves as a unique forum for dialogue and exchange. It brings together institutions, labor unions, development cooperation actors, civil society organizati
items.titleIt: Ambassador to Senegal Opens Goreé School for Sustainable Migration

items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Confindustria sounds alarm about outlook amid fresh conflict in the Gulf
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 24 - The CSC research unit of Italian industry association Confindustria on Friday sounded the alarm about the nation's economic outlook following a breakdown of the Iran war ceasefire. "The third quarter is off to a bad start," the CSC said in its latest economic outlook report. "The war in Iran flared up again in July, and uncertainty about future developments is at a peak: oil prices were immediately affected. "Having already slowed Italian GDP in the second quarter, the war's impact is now spilling over into the third,. "Inflation will remain high for some time and credi
items.titleIt: Confindustria sounds alarm about outlook amid fresh conflict in the Gulf

items.title: Italy’s SACE and Bank of Africa in Rabat Join Forces for the Mattei Plan
items.description: SACE, the Italian Export Credit Agency owned by the Ministry of Economy and Finance, and Bank of Africa, one of the leading banking groups on the African continent, have signed an agreement to invest jointly in Africa and promote the involvement of Italian companies in strategic projects being carried out on the continent. items.titleIt: Italy’s SACE and Bank of Africa in Rabat Join Forces for the Mattei Plan

items.title: No rush to find new Italy coach says Maldini
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 23 - The Italian Soccer Federation's (FIGC) new Technical Director Paolo Maldini said Thursday that he was not in a rush to appoint a new coach for the national team. Earlier this week FIGC President Giovanni Malagò confirmed reports that the federation had approached Pep Guardiola about taking on the role and Maldini added on Thursday that they have looked up Brazil coach Carlo Ancelotti too. "Ideally, it would be this week, but, above all, we need to wait for the person we really want," Maldini said after he, Malagò and FIGC consultant Leonardo had a meeting with Serie A c
items.titleIt: No rush to find new Italy coach says Maldini

items.title: Ethiopia: U.S.-Ethiopia Public Private Dialogue to Benefit U.S. and Ethiopian Businesses and Economic Growth
items.description: [U.S. Embassy Addis Ababa] Addis Ababa -- U.S. Ambassador to Ethiopia Ervin J. Massinga co-chaired a Public-Private Dialogue (PPD) with Ethiopian Investment Commission (EIC) Commissioner Dr. Zeleke Temesgen and Chief Macroeconomic Advisor to the Prime Minister Ambassador Girma Biru at the Skylight Hotel in Addis Ababa on July 23. The dialogue focused on advancing commercial diplomacy, expanding market access, and ensuring a level playing field for U.S. companies operating in Ethiopia. By bringing together representatives from the American Chamber
items.titleIt: Ethiopia: U.S.-Ethiopia Public Private Dialogue to Benefit U.S. and Ethiopian Businesses and Economic Growth

items.description: [Dabanga] Amsterdam / Kampala -- The 11th voluntary return flight carrying Sudanese refugees from Uganda to Sudan departed on Thursday with 80 returnees on board.
